## [Math63 v1.5 → v1.6 + Math64 v1.0 (NEW) — §2A.3 Stage α CLOSED: Corollary 2A.3 promoted to window-level (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Theory]
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.5 → v1.6. Header Status updated to "§§2A.1/2A.2/2A.3 addenda; §2A.3 Stage $\alpha$ CLOSED — Corollary 2A.3 promoted to window-level". Header changelog gains a v1.5 → v1.6 block documenting the Trigger (live Stage $\alpha$ four-configuration sweep on user local machine, R-2026-04-22-003), Evidence (four per-config aggregate tables at $\mu^{2}\in\{-1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1\}$; $a_{\alpha_A} = a_{\alpha_B} = 4.35668\times 10^{-7}$ bit-identical to ten decimal places under FD and `torch.func.jvp` backends; $a_{\alpha_D}(\mathrm{FULL}) = 1.421\times 10^{-14}$ under structural cII ablation, two decades below the $10^{-12}$ threshold), and Decision (promote Corollary 2A.3 from single-state to window-level; close Stage $\alpha$; reformulate §2D gate as absolute bound; file Math64 companion audit note).
- New §2A.3 subsubsection **"Stage $\alpha$ CLOSED: sole-carrier claim promoted to window-level under two backends and structural ablation (2026-04-22)"** with: boxed Eq. `math63-2A3-stage-alpha-result` tabulating $(\alpha_A, \alpha_B, \alpha_C, \alpha_D) = (4.3567\times 10^{-7}, 4.3567\times 10^{-7}, 1.4211\times 10^{-14}, 1.4211\times 10^{-14})$ at every $\mu^{2}$; **Theorem `math63-2A3-stage-alpha-closure`** with four-point statement (i) non-cII blocks $\le 10^{-13}$ at Hermitian precision floor across all configurations and all six $\mu^{2}$; (ii) $a_{\mathrm{cII}} = 4.357\times 10^{-7}$ backend-invariant (FD-vs-autograd agreement to ten digits); (iii) $a_{\mathrm{FULL}} = a_{\mathrm{cII}}$ under the absolute-norm identity; (iv) cII-off structural ablation collapses $a_{\mathrm{FULL}} \to 1.421\times 10^{-14}$; proof includes **FD antisym-invariance remark** (central-FD truncation $D^{3}F[v,v,v]/6$ is a symmetric trilinear form by Clairaut's theorem, contributing only to the Hermitian part of the Jacobian, which explains the 10-digit FD/autograd agreement structurally rather than as a coincidence).
- **Corollary `math63-2A3-cII-state-independence`**: $a_{\mathrm{cII}}(\Psi)$ is $\mu^{2}$-independent across the continuation window; the original $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ Stage $[4/4]$ signal is re-classified as a **Phase D PLACEHOLDER iterate-norm artefact** — the relative ratio $a_{\mathrm{cII}}/\|\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{full}}\|_{F}$ was polluted by a small Phase D pseudo-iterate denominator, not by a physical $\mu^{2}$-dependent anti-Hermitian scaling.
- CLI invocation note paragraph: both `--mu2-list=-1.0,...` and `--mu2-list -1.0,...` forms are now accepted as of tool v1.2.1.
- Scope remark updated with Stage $\alpha$ CLOSED line; (D3) subsumed by $\alpha_B$; (D4) subsumed by $\alpha_C$/$\alpha_D$; (D2) and (D5) retained.
- `docs/math/TECT-Math64-cII-Stage-Alpha-Audit.tex.txt` **v1.0 (NEW, ~450 lines)**, the companion audit note for R-2026-04-22-003. Contents: (§1) raw data — four per-config aggregate tables + full-precision seven-row table at $\mu^{2}=-1.0$; (§2) Stage $\alpha$ acceptance-gate evaluation — Eq. `stage-alpha-gate`, Theorem `math64-stage-alpha-closure`, Corollary `math64-cII-state-indep`; (§3) Wirtinger analysis — **Lemma `math64-fd-antisym`** (central-FD truncation is antisym-invariant by Clairaut on symmetric $D^{3}F$), Wirtinger decomposition Eq. `cII-antisym-decomp` with structural non-closedness of the projected-divergence operator $\nabla\cdot(\Pi_{\mathrm{channel}}(T\Psi - q\Psi))$ identified as the mathematical origin; (§4) **§2D acceptance-gate reformulation** — Eq. `sec2d-gate` replaces the relative ratio with the absolute bound $a_{\mathrm{cII}}(\Psi) \le \tau_{\mathrm{struct}} \in [10^{-6}, 10^{-5}]$; (§5) three resolutions — **(X) in-solver symmetrisation** $\tilde{\mathcal{J}} = \tfrac{1}{2}(\mathcal{J} + \mathcal{J}^{\dagger})$ on the preconditioned operator (recommended path for v2.6.0 Phase D integration; O(1) overhead per application), **(Y) Euler-Lagrange rewrite** with a scalar functional $\Phi_{\mathrm{cII}}[\Psi]$ whose Fréchet derivative is the symmetrised cII residual (candidate Math65 rigorous long-term path; Helmholtz-Hodge obstruction noted — the projected divergence is not automatically closed on the channel subspace, so $\Phi_{\mathrm{cII}}$ may not exist as a single-valued functional without a compatibility condition), **(Z) unconditional FGMRES** (not recommended — abandons self-adjoint preconditioning and the $\{$PCG, MINRES, FGMRES$\}$ routing policy); (§6) confirmatory items — (D1) CLOSED, (D2) retained, (D3) subsumed by $\alpha_B$, (D4) subsumed by $\alpha_C$/$\alpha_D$, (D5) retained for N-scaling $\tau_{\mathrm{struct}}$ calibration at $N\in\{16, 32, 48, 64\}$; (§7) conclusion.

### [Code]
- None (Stage $\alpha$ execution used tool v1.2.1 already landed; no source changes accompany this closure).

### [Results]
- **R-2026-04-22-003** filed. Stage $\alpha$ four-configuration sweep on N=32 BCC, $\mu^{2}\in\{-1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1\}$, five probes per point, seed 42, $\sigma=10^{-2}$:
  - $\alpha_A$ (FD, cII on): $a_{\mathrm{cII}} = 4.35668\times 10^{-7}$ at every $\mu^{2}$; $a_{\mathrm{FULL}} = 4.35668\times 10^{-7}$.
  - $\alpha_B$ (autograd `torch.func.jvp`, cII on): $a_{\mathrm{cII}} = 4.35668\times 10^{-7}$ at every $\mu^{2}$ (identical to $\alpha_A$ to ten digits).
  - $\alpha_C$ (FD, cII off): $a_{\mathrm{FULL}} = 1.4211\times 10^{-14}$.
  - $\alpha_D$ (autograd, cII off): $a_{\mathrm{FULL}} = 1.4211\times 10^{-14}$.
  - Non-cII blocks: $a_{\mathrm{bra}}, a_{\mathrm{fam}}, a_{\mathrm{lock}}, a_{\mathrm{nl}} \le 10^{-13}$; $a_{\mathrm{shell}} = 0$ (dormant, $\eta_{\mathrm{shell}} = 0$ in config).
- **Acceptance-gate `math63-2A3-stage-alpha-gate` satisfied**: $|a_{\alpha_A} - a_{\alpha_B}| = 0$ (saturates the $10^{-9}$ threshold by ten-digit bit-identity); $a_{\alpha_D}(\mathrm{FULL}) = 1.421\times 10^{-14} \le 10^{-12}$ (two decades below threshold).
- **Verdict**: Corollary 2A.3 (sole-carrier) is **promoted from "empirically verified on one state under FD" to "empirically verified across the continuation window under two independent backends and under structural ablation"**. Hypotheses S2 (hidden inter-block coupling) and S3 (Nyquist-mode aliasing) are window-level **falsified**; hypothesis S1 (structural non-self-adjointness of the projected-divergence Class II block) is window-level **confirmed**.

### [Docs]
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §11 revision history: new top row summarising Math63 v1.5 → v1.6 (Theorem, Corollary, FD antisym-invariance remark) + Math64 v1.0 (NEW companion note) + Task #109 promotion + §2D gate reformulation.
- CHANGELOG 2026-04-22 top entry (this entry).

### [Infrastructure]
- None.

### Retires
- The v1.5 Stage $\alpha$ "plan sealed, live execution pending" status — superseded by v1.6 "Stage $\alpha$ CLOSED, Corollary 2A.3 promoted to window-level" after successful execution of R-2026-04-22-003.
- The §2D acceptance-gate formulation as a **relative ratio** $a_{\mathrm{cII}}/\|\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{full}}\|_{F}$ — superseded by the **absolute bound** Eq. `sec2d-gate` $a_{\mathrm{cII}}(\Psi) \le \tau_{\mathrm{struct}}$ in Math64 §4, which is robust to the Phase D iterate-norm artefact that produced the misleading $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ Stage $[4/4]$ signal.
- The (D3) autograd-vs-FD probe step as a stand-alone confirmatory item — subsumed by $\alpha_B$; the $\alpha_A$-$\alpha_B$ 10-digit agreement is explained structurally by Lemma `math64-fd-antisym`.
- The (D4) Nyquist-mode-ablation step as a stand-alone confirmatory item — subsumed by the structural $\alpha_C$/$\alpha_D$ cII-off ablation, which is strictly sharper (removes the entire Class II sector, not merely its Nyquist modes).

### Math note
Primary: `docs/math/TECT-Math64-cII-Stage-Alpha-Audit.tex.txt` v1.0 (NEW). Secondary: `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.5 → v1.6 (Stage $\alpha$ CLOSED subsubsection).

### Residual behaviour
Phase D of v2.5 remains a PLACEHOLDER (exit $10$ / `SKELETON_ONLY`) until Task #104 (v2.6.0 Phase D wiring) lands. Task #104 is now **unblocked**: the §2D acceptance-gate is specified (Eq. `sec2d-gate` absolute bound), the operator-level treatment of the anti-Hermitian cII sector is specified (Math64 §5 resolution (X) in-solver symmetrisation), and $\tau_{\mathrm{struct}}$ calibration is the only open numerical item (Math64 §6 (D5), N-scaling test at $N\in\{16,32,48,64\}$). A follow-on Math65 note is proposed to decide between (X) symmetrisation and (Y) Euler-Lagrange rewrite as the rigorous long-term operator structure for the cII sector.

## [check_jacobian_blocks v1.2 → v1.2.1 — argparse negative-list CLI usability hotfix (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Code]
- `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.2 → v1.2.1. Python's `argparse` refuses to accept a value that begins with `-` (e.g. `-1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1`) as the argument of a string-typed option when given in the space-separated form `--mu2-list <neg-csv>`, because argparse cannot disambiguate between "option-value that happens to look negative" and "an unknown optional flag". The R-2026-04-22 Stage $\alpha$ driver invocation `python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py --backend fd --mu2-list -1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1` hit exactly this failure: `error: argument --mu2-list: expected one argument`.
- **Fix** (v1.2.1): new helper `_glue_negative_list_value(argv, option_name)` preprocesses `sys.argv[1:]` inside `main()` before `parser.parse_args(...)` runs. A token matching `option_name` (currently only `--mu2-list`) whose immediate successor looks like a negative numerical value (`startswith("-")` and contains a digit, a comma, or `.digit`) is folded into the equals-sign form `option_name=<value>` as a single token. All other token patterns, including already-equals-sign-formatted invocations and positive-numerical CSVs, are left untouched.
- **Verification**: AST-parse clean; `--selftest` retained 5/5 PASS; new 6/6 glue-function unit test covers (i) negative CSV space-separated, (ii) negative CSV space-separated with other flags, (iii) already-equals-sign form, (iv) positive CSV space-separated, (v) single-negative value, (vi) unrelated flags with `--cII-off`.
- **Back-compat**: the equals-sign form `--mu2-list=-1.0,...` is bit-identical under v1.2.1; positive-CSV and single-positive forms are untouched; v1.2 helper APIs (`_resolve_torch_jvp`, `_params_with_cII_off`, `run_multi_mu2_sweep`, `_print_single_point_report`, `_exit_code_from_sweep_report`) are preserved verbatim.

### [Theory]
- None (CLI ergonomics only; no change to Math63 §2A.1 probe policy or §2A.3 Stage $\alpha$ acceptance condition).

### [Results]
- None (blocker removal; live execution resumes on user machine).

### [Docs]

## [Math63 v1.4 → v1.5 + check_jacobian_blocks v1.1 → v1.2 — §2A.3 Stage α plan sealed (autograd / multi-mu2 / cII-off extension) (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Code]
- `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.1 → v1.2. Three orthogonal CLI flags added:
  - **`--backend {fd,autograd}`** routes the cII Jacobian-vector product through either the pre-existing central-finite-difference path at $\varepsilon_{\mathrm{classII\_hess}} = 5\times 10^{-7}$ (matching `backend.hessian_vec`) or a forward-mode JVP via `torch.func.jvp`, with `torch.autograd.functional.jvp` as graceful fallback for PyTorch $<2.0$. The autograd path evaluates $(d/dt)|_{t=0}F_{\mathrm{cII}}(\Psi + t\,v) = A(\Psi)v + B(\Psi)\overline{v}$ exactly on `complex128`, removing the central-FD $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^{2})\sim 10^{-13}$ truncation floor and the $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^{-1}u_{\mathrm{round}})\sim 2\times 10^{-10}$ cancellation noise that v1.1 carried.
  - **`--mu2-list CSV`** replaces the single `--mu2 VALUE` driver with a loop over a list of continuation points, each with its own fresh thermal BCC seed via `math56_constants.build_seed_bcc`. The canonical Stage $\alpha$ list mirrors the original R-2026-04-22 signal window: $\mu^{2} \in \{-1.0, -0.8, -0.6, -0.4, -0.2, -0.1\}$.
  - **`--cII-off`** zeros $\{\alpha_{X}, \beta_{X}, c_{JJ}, c_{JK}\}$ in a `params` CLONE (shallow copy, caller params untouched), prunes `cII` from the block sweep, and re-runs `backend.hessian_vec` on the ablated params. This is a structural, not statistical, test; it leverages the existing early-exit guard at line 430 of `real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3._classII_effective_term_t` ($\sum|\text{coupling}| < 10^{-18} \Rightarrow$ `torch.zeros_like`).
- **Back-compat**: the single-`--mu2` driver is preserved identically to v1.1; `--backend` defaults to `fd`, `--cII-off` defaults to False. Every pre-v1.2 invocation is bit-identical under v1.2.
- **AST-parse clean; self-test retained 5/5 PASS** at Hermitian precision floor `antisym/|J| = {3.95e-17, 0.0, 8.98e-17, 5.05e-01, 5.39e-17}` for (diag-real-PD, diag-real-indef, Hermitian-complex, anti-Hermitian-complex, nl-Wirtinger). Live Stage $\alpha$ four-configuration sweep (backend $\times$ cII-on/off $\times$ 6-point $\mu^{2}$ list) is wall-clock-bounded at $\sim 5$ min on $N=32$ BCC PyTorch CPU backend.
- **Helpers added (v1.2)**:
  - `_resolve_torch_jvp()` — returns a cached shim that prefers `torch.func.jvp` and falls back to `torch.autograd.functional.jvp`; reports the routed backend in the per-block report's `cII_backend_applied` field.
  - `_params_with_cII_off(params)` — the zero-clone constructor (idempotent, shallow copy).
  - `run_multi_mu2_sweep(...)` — driver looping `run_block_sweep` over `mu2_list` with a fresh BCC seed per point.
  - `_print_single_point_report(...)`, `_exit_code_from_sweep_report(...)` — formatter + exit-code helpers extracted from `main()` for reuse across single-point and multi-$\mu^{2}$ paths.

### [Theory]
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.4 → v1.5. Header Status updated to "Specification (sealed + §§2A.1/2A.2/2A.3 addenda; §2A.3 Step (D1) executed, S1 confirmed; §2A.3 Stage α plan sealed)".
- Header changelog gains a full v1.4 → v1.5 block documenting the Trigger (three residual degrees of freedom in (D1)-only sole-carrier claim: FD-vs-autograd, single-$\mu^{2}$-vs-window, statistical-vs-structural-ablation), Evidence (FD truncation $\sim 10^{-13}$ lies 6 decades below the $4.357\times 10^{-7}$ signal — unlikely but not excluded), and Decision (Stage $\alpha$ four-configuration sweep, tool v1.2 extension spec).
- New §2A.3 subsubsection **"§2A.3 Stage $\alpha$: orthogonal confirmation of the sole-carrier claim (plan, 2026-04-22)"** inserted before the Scope remark, with:
  - Tool-extension paragraph naming the three CLI flags and their operator-level semantics.
  - Execution matrix Eq. `math63-2A3-stage-alpha-matrix` tabulating configurations $(\alpha_{\mathrm{A}},\alpha_{\mathrm{B}},\alpha_{\mathrm{C}},\alpha_{\mathrm{D}})$ and expected per-$\mu^{2}$ outcomes ($a(\mathrm{cII})\approx 4.36\times 10^{-7}$ under backend $\{$fd, autograd$\}$ with cII on; $a(\text{FULL})\lesssim 10^{-13}$ with cII off).
  - Acceptance gate Eq. `math63-2A3-stage-alpha-gate`: $|a_{\alpha_{\mathrm{A}}} - a_{\alpha_{\mathrm{B}}}| \le 10^{-9}$ and $a_{\alpha_{\mathrm{D}}}(\text{FULL}) \le 10^{-12}$ at all six continuation points simultaneously; satisfaction promotes Corollary 2A.3 from "empirically verified on one state under FD" to "empirically verified across the continuation window under two independent backends and under structural ablation".
  - Three-item fallback ladder (FD artefact $\to$ non-cII residual leakage $\to$ $\mu^{2}$-dependent scaling) in order of decreasing a priori likelihood.
  - Stage $\alpha$ report destination: `docs/math/TECT-Math64-cII-Stage-Alpha-Audit.tex.txt` (to be filed upon execution); JSON artefacts under `results/stage_alpha_{A,B,C,D}_{fd,autograd}_{on,off}.json`.
- Scope remark updated with the Stage $\alpha$ plan-sealed line (2026-04-22, v1.5, tool v1.2 AST-parse clean + self-test 5/5 PASS, live execution pending user local machine).

Expected Stage $\alpha$ outcome under hypothesis S1: four bit-identical $6\times 7$ tables (fd / autograd) showing `cII_antisym_abs ≈ 4.36e-7` across all $\mu^{2}$ and `FULL_antisym_abs ≈ 4.36e-7` with cII on / $\le 10^{-12}$ with cII off. Any deviation triggers the fallback ladder.

## [Math63 v1.3 → v1.4 — §2A.3 Step (D1) live execution: S1 empirically confirmed, F_cII isolated as sole carrier of the anti-Hermitian component (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Results]
- **Step (D1) live sweep executed** on user local machine via `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.1 with `(N, μ², n_probes, σ, seed) = (32, -0.5, 5, 10^{-2}, 42)` on `PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json`. Per-block relative antisymmetry $\operatorname{antisym}/\lVert\mathcal{J}_X\rVert_F$:
  - $F_{\mathrm{bra}}$: $3.97\times 10^{-18}$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 2.69\times 10^{+3}$), SYM-PD.
  - $F_{\mathrm{fam}}$: $1.23\times 10^{-18}$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 4.41\times 10^{-2}$), SYM-PD.
  - $F_{\mathrm{lock}}$: $3.54\times 10^{-18}$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 1.23\times 10^{-1}$), SYM-PD.
  - $F_{\mathrm{shell}}$: $0$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 0$, dormant: $\eta_{\mathrm{shell}} = 0$ in config).
  - $F_{\mathrm{nl}}$: $4.60\times 10^{-18}$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 2.30\times 10^{-4}$), SYM-IND.
  - $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$: **$5.84\times 10^{-3}$** ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 7.46\times 10^{-5}$), **ASYM** (exceeds threshold $10^{-8}$ by factor $5.84\times 10^{+5}$).
  - FULL: $1.62\times 10^{-10}$ ($\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert_F = 2.69\times 10^{+3}$), SYM-PD (below threshold on the thermal seed).
- **Absolute anti-Hermitian norm isolation identity** (new Eq. `math63-2A3-cII-isolation` of Math63 v1.4):
  $\lVert\mathcal{J}_{\text{full}} - \mathcal{J}_{\text{full}}^{\dagger}\rVert_F = \lVert\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{cII}} - \mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{cII}}^{\dagger}\rVert_F = 4.35668\times 10^{-7}$, with the two sides agreeing to nine decimal places (numerically: ratio $= 0.99999999431$).
- **Hypothesis S1 (structural non-self-adjointness of $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$) empirically confirmed.** Hypotheses S2 (hidden inter-block coupling) and S3 (Nyquist-mode aliasing) are falsified on the probed state — both would require compensating or localised anti-Hermitian contributions from blocks other than cII, ruled out by the five Hermitian-floor classifications.
- **Explanation of the original $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ signal**. The ratio $\operatorname{antisym}/\lVert\mathcal{J}\rVert$ is state-dependent through the denominator $\lVert\mathcal{J}_{\text{full}}\rVert_F$:
  - thermal seed (N=32, μ²=−0.5): $\lVert\mathcal{J}_{\text{full}}\rVert_F \approx 2.7\times 10^{+3}$ → ratio $= 1.62\times 10^{-10}$;
  - Stage [4/4] Newton pseudo-iterate (v2.5.7 Phase D PLACEHOLDER): $\lVert\mathcal{J}_{\text{full}}\rVert_F \approx 1.6\times 10^{-3}$ → ratio $= 4.36\times 10^{-7} / 1.6\times 10^{-3} \approx 2.7\times 10^{-4}$.
  The six-$\mu^{2}$-point identity of the signal is thus a consequence of Phase D producing similar-scale pseudo-iterates at each continuation point, not a physical property of the Math63 continuation trajectory.
- **Secondary finding — shell dormancy**. Block norm $\lVert\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{shell}}\rVert_F = 0$ exactly because `eta_shell = 0.0` in `PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json`. Lemma 2A.3-1 remains valid and will be re-confirmed once $\eta_{\mathrm{shell}}>0$ is activated in a future Math56-Addendum revision.
- Numerical artefacts: `results/step1_block_probe.json` on user machine; `PS C:\Dev\TECT2\Contents>` stderr log captured in the session transcript.

### [Theory]
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.3 → v1.4. §2A.3 body extended with:
  - Paragraph **Step (D1) execution result (2026-04-22 live run)** with explicit seven-row table (six blocks + FULL) and Eq. `math63-2A3-cII-isolation` for the absolute anti-Hermitian norm identity.
  - **Corollary 2A.3 (Sole carrier of the anti-Hermitian component)**: on the thermal seed, $\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{cII}}$ is the unique anti-Hermitian block; S1 confirmed, S2 and S3 falsified; Lemmas 2A.3-1 and 2A.3-2 promoted from "a priori" to "a priori + empirically verified at $10^{-18}$".
  - **Remark (State dependence of the $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ signal)**: explicit state-norm decomposition, explanation of $\mu^{2}$-independence via PLACEHOLDER Phase D.
  - **Remark (Secondary finding: shell block dormancy)**.
  - **Remark (Restatement of residual plan after (D1) closure)**: priority order for the remaining steps is now **(D3-on-cII) $\succ$ (D5) $\succ$ (D2) $\succ$ (D4)**; (D4) demoted to OPTIONAL contingent on (D5) outcome.
  - Scope remark updated: "Step (D1) closed 2026-04-22 (S1 confirmed), Steps (D2)–(D5) retained as confirmatory".
- Header v1.3 → v1.4 status: "Specification (sealed + §§2A.1/2A.2/2A.3 addenda; §2A.3 Step (D1) executed, S1 confirmed)"; full v1.3→v1.4 change-history block inserted documenting the live-run numerics and the S1-confirmation decision.

### [Code]

Tool is retained unchanged; Step (D3)/(D5) will re-use it with modified arguments.

### [Docs]
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §11 revision history: new top row summarising Math63 v1.3 → v1.4 + Step (D1) live closure, Task #109.

### [Infrastructure]
- None.

### Retires
- Hypotheses S2 (hidden inter-block anti-Hermitian coupling) and S3 (Nyquist-mode aliasing on BCC) — falsified at the block level on the probed state.
- The phrasing "2.66·10^{−4} is a puzzling $\mu^{2}$-independent signal" — now resolved as `4.36·10^{−7} / ‖J_full‖` with a PLACEHOLDER-Phase-D-dependent denominator.

### Math note
`TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.3 → v1.4, §2A.3 post-execution block (Corollary 2A.3, state-dependence remark, shell-dormancy remark, plan restatement).

### Residual behaviour
Phase D of v2.5 remains a PLACEHOLDER; §2A.3 continues to be orthogonal to v2.6 wiring. The immediate next action is **Step (D3-on-cII)**: autograd cross-check of $\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{cII}}$ against the current `eps=5e-7` central-FD JVP, to discriminate between
- **(D3-α)** genuine structural non-self-adjointness of the projected-divergence Class II residual (expected: $4.36\times 10^{-7}$ signal persists under autograd, forcing a Class-II rewrite either as an EL variation of an explicit scalar functional, or as an operator-level symmetrisation `(J_cII + J_cII^{\dagger})/2`);
- **(D3-β)** central-FD truncation artefact (expected: signal drops to Hermitian floor under autograd; current implementation can be retained with a documented `eps` bound).
Scenario (D3-α) is the a priori likely outcome given the projected-divergence structure is not an Euler–Lagrange variation; (D3-β) is the falsifiable alternative whose rejection will cement the remediation requirement.

## [check_jacobian_blocks v1.0 → v1.1 — sys.path bootstrap hotfix for Step (D1) live execution (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Code]
- `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.0 → v1.1. First live invocation aborted at `_build_seed()` line 499 with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'math56_constants'`. Root cause: v1.0 relied on ambient `sys.path`, but `math56_constants.py` and `real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3.py` both live in `Contents/PDE/`, which is not on `sys.path` when the script is launched from `Contents/` via `python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py ...`. This is the exact analogue of the `continuation_mu2_v25.py` v2.5.0 → v2.5.1 fix (Task #100) and belongs to the same class of import-bootstrap errors that the v2.0 probe tool already resolved. Fix: insert a `sys.path` bootstrap block immediately after the stdlib imports (lines 105–122 of v1.1) that uses `os.path.abspath(__file__)` as the anchor to compute `_THIS_FILE_DIR = .../Contents/Tools`, `_REPO_ROOT_DIR = .../Contents`, `_PDE_DIR = .../Contents/PDE`, and prepends both `_PDE_DIR` and `_REPO_ROOT_DIR` to `sys.path` if not already present. Both `from math56_constants import build_seed_bcc` and `importlib.import_module('real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3')` now resolve deterministically regardless of launch directory. Patch is import-time only; the probe logic, Wirtinger JVP, and self-test harness are bit-identical to v1.0.

### [Theory]
- No change. Math63 v1.3 §2A.3 specification is plumbing-invariant.

### [Docs]
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.5 MATURITY block header v1.0 → v1.1; new v1.1 change-note entry inserted above the v1.0 entry, documenting the root-cause trace, the Task #100 analogue, the scope of the patch, and the 5/5 PASS self-test retention.
- §11 revision history: new top row summarising the v1.1 hotfix, Task #109.

### [Results]
- AST-parse clean after patch. `python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py --selftest` returns 5/5 PASS with antisym/‖J‖ identical to v1.0:
  `{3.95·10^{−17}, 0.0, 8.98·10^{−17}, 5.05·10^{−01}, 5.39·10^{−17}}`
  for (diag-real-PD, diag-real-indef, Hermitian-complex, anti-Hermitian-complex iH, nl-Wirtinger). The bootstrap is a no-op along the self-test path (which does not touch backend modules), confirming that the patch is strictly plumbing.

### [Infrastructure]
- None. No new files, no new dependencies.

### Retires
- Ambient-sys.path assumption in `check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.0 (single-release defect).

### Math note
`TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` unchanged (still v1.3 with §2A.3 addendum).

### Residual behaviour
Phase D of v2.5 is still a PLACEHOLDER; §2A.3 is orthogonal. The Step (D1) live sweep is now immediately runnable with the original CLI:
```bash
python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py \
    --config PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json \
    --N 32 --mu2 -0.5 --n-probes 5 \
    --output results/step1_block_probe.json --verbose
```

## [check_jacobian_blocks v1.0 — Math63 §2A.3 BCC Jacobian Residual Anti-Hermitian Component Diagnostic, Step (D1) landing (Task #109)] — 2026-04-22

### [Code]
- `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.0 **created** (~460 lines). Sibling to `Tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v2.0 (which is retained unchanged as the full-residual reference); does **not** supersede it. Implements Step (D1) of the Math63 §2A.3 diagnostic plan: the operator-level block decomposition probe. Six additive residual blocks of `real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3.residual()` are probed **in isolation** under the v2.0 complex-Hermitian protocol (real-self-adjoint criterion $\operatorname{Re}\langle u, \mathcal{J}v\rangle = \operatorname{Re}\langle \mathcal{J}u, v\rangle$, complex $\mathcal{CN}(0,I)$ probes, `torch.vdot`/`np.vdot` sesquilinear inner product, threshold $\operatorname{antisym}/\lVert J\rVert < 10^{-8}$).
  - Block registry: `{"bra"=F_bra, "fam"=F_fam, "lock"=F_lock, "shell"=F_shell, "nl"=F_nl, "cII"=F_cII}` following the additive decomposition `F = F_bra + F_fam + F_lock + F_shell + F_nl + F_cII` (Definition 2A.3-1 of Math63 v1.3).
  - `F_nl` (quartic + sextic) uses the **analytical Wirtinger JVP** from Lemma 2A.3-2, not a finite-difference approximation: `delta_rho = 2·Re(Ψ* · v)`, `dq = λ(ρ·v + δρ·Ψ)`, `ds = γ(ρ²·v + 2ρ·δρ·Ψ)`. Reference form `A(Ψ) v = (λ + 2γρ)ρ v + (λ + 2γρ) (Ψ Ψ*) v` + `B(Ψ) v̄ = (λ + 2γρ)diag(Ψ_i²)·v̄` from the Wirtinger decomposition proven real-self-adjoint in §2A.3.
  - `F_cII` (Class-II divergence term) uses central finite difference with `eps=5e-7`, matching `backend.hessian_vec` convention. This block is flagged **a priori structurally suspicious** — a projected divergence `(TΨ − qΨ)` with channel projector is **not** the Euler–Lagrange variation of any single scalar functional (Remark 2A.3 of Math63 v1.3, S1/S2/S3 scenarios).
  - CLI: `python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py --config PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json --N <N> --mu2 <mu2> --n-probes 5 --output results/step1_block_probe.json [--blocks bra,fam,lock,shell,nl,cII] [--seed 42] [--verbose] [--selftest]`. Exit code $0$ iff **all** requested blocks classify symmetric; exit code $11$ otherwise. `--selftest` intercepted before argparse so it runs even in torch-less environments (numpy-only fallback).
- `Tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v2.0 — **no code change**. Reused as (a) the v2.0 complex-Hermitian probe harness (`probe_symmetry` is called internally by the block wrappers), (b) the full-residual reference that the new tool diffs against for consistency (`F_full = sum_X F_X` must reproduce the previously reported `antisym/‖J‖ = 2.66·10^{−4}` signature).

### [Theory]
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.2 → v1.3. **§2A.3 addendum "BCC Jacobian Residual Anti-Hermitian Component Diagnostic Framework" inserted between §2A.2 and §B**. New content:
  - **Motivating empirical input** (Eq. `math63-2A3-signal`): the first live Stage [4/4] run under v2.0 probe policy reported $\operatorname{antisym}/\lVert J\rVert = 2.66\times 10^{-4}$ **identical across all six $\mu^{2}$ continuation points**. This signal is $10^{13}$ above the Hermitian precision floor ($\sim 10^{-17}$), $10^{3}$ below the full anti-Hermitian level ($\sim 0.5$), and $\mu^{2}$-independent — pointing to a **structural** (not noise-level) anti-Hermitian defect in one of the residual blocks.
  - **Definition 2A.3-1** (Block decomposition). The BCC residual is additively decomposed into six blocks $F = F_{\mathrm{bra}} + F_{\mathrm{fam}} + F_{\mathrm{lock}} + F_{\mathrm{shell}} + F_{\mathrm{nl}} + F_{\mathrm{cII}}$, each written out explicitly.
  - **Lemma 2A.3-1** (Hermiticity of linear blocks). Proves $F_{\mathrm{bra}}$, $F_{\mathrm{fam}}$, $F_{\mathrm{lock}}$, $F_{\mathrm{shell}}$ are real-self-adjoint under $\operatorname{Re}\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle$ by showing each is a pointwise multiplier or BCC spectral operator with real symbol in $k$-space (body-diagonal weights $w_j$ satisfying $\sum_j w_j \hat{e}_j^i \hat{e}_j^k = \frac{c}{3}\delta^{ik}$ isotropy condition).
  - **Lemma 2A.3-2** (Real-self-adjointness of $F_{\mathrm{nl}}$ under $\operatorname{Re}\langle\cdot,\cdot\rangle$). Constructs the Wirtinger split $\mathcal{J}_{\mathrm{nl}} v = A(\Psi) v + B(\Psi)\overline{v}$ with $A(\Psi) = (\lambda + 2\gamma\rho)\rho\,\mathbb{I} + \lambda\Psi\Psi^{*} + 2\gamma\rho\Psi\Psi^{*}$ (manifestly $A = A^{\dagger}$) and $B(\Psi) = (\lambda + 2\gamma\rho)\operatorname{diag}(\Psi_i^{2})$ (symmetric, $B = B^{T}$). Proves $\operatorname{Re}\langle u, Av + B\overline{v}\rangle = \operatorname{Re}\langle Au + B\overline{u}, v\rangle$ via the Wirtinger identity $\operatorname{Re}\langle u, B\overline{v}\rangle = \operatorname{Re}\langle B\overline{u}, v\rangle$ for any symmetric $B$.
  - **Remark 2A.3** (Residual suspicion window). With five of six blocks proven real-self-adjoint a priori, the $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ signal is localised to $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$ up to three scenarios: **(S1)** $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$ is genuinely non-self-adjoint as implemented — a projected-divergence structure $\nabla\cdot(\Pi_{\text{channel}}(T\Psi - q\Psi))$ is **not** the Euler–Lagrange variation of a single scalar functional; **(S2)** a hidden non-Hermitian coupling between blocks (e.g. a shared projector evaluated on stale state); **(S3)** a Nyquist-mode (k = N/2) lattice artefact of the BCC spectral operator at finite $a$.
  - **Definition 2A.3 Plan** (D1)–(D5): (D1) operator-level block decomposition probe — the deliverable of this landing; (D2) simple-cubic vs BCC cross-check (isolates lattice-geometry origin); (D3) analytic vs autograd Jacobian cross-check (isolates implementation origin); (D4) Nyquist-mode ablation (isolates aliasing origin); (D5) N-scaling test at $N \in \{16, 32, 48, 64\}$ (isolates continuum-limit behaviour).
  - **Remark (Acceptance-gate implication for §2D)**. If the diagnostic localises the signal to a specific block and S1/S2 proves to be the correct scenario, the §2D acceptance tolerance must be tightened to require block-level symmetry, not merely full-residual symmetry — otherwise a compensating anti-Hermitian pair across blocks could pass the full-residual probe while hiding a structural defect.
  - **Scope remark**. Applies to `PDE/real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3.residual`; the scalar-Brazovskii backend is excluded (no BCC spectral Laplacian, no Class-II term).
- Header change-history v1.2 → v1.3 block added with full Trigger (identical $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ signal across six $\mu^{2}$ points under v2.0 probe) / Evidence (Lemmas 2A.3-1, 2A.3-2 exhaust 5 of 6 blocks) / Decision (Definition 2A.3 Plan (D1)–(D5)).

### [Docs]
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` **§10.5 added** for `Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.0. Documented: purpose, non-supersession relation to §10.3 (sibling), function signatures (`probe_block_symmetry`, `run_block_sweep`, `_build_block_residuals`, `_build_block_jvp`, `_self_test`), inputs/outputs, CLI, dependencies, Math63 §2A.3 pointer, per-block theoretical prediction table (Hermitian floor $\sim 10^{-17}$ for bra/fam/lock/shell/nl; S1 predicts $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$ isolates the $2.66\times 10^{-4}$ signal), accept-symmetric threshold, MATURITY (SKELETON-EXECUTABLE diagnostic tier; self-test 5/5 PASS at Hermitian floor), v1.0 change notes documenting the Case 2 balanced-diagonal fix.
- §11 revision history table: new top row summarising the `check_jacobian_blocks.py` v1.0 landing + Math63 v1.2 → v1.3 §2A.3 addendum, Task #109.

### [Results]
- No new live numerical results. Step (D1) sandbox-side deliverable is **complete**: (a) tool AST-parse clean; (b) self-test 5/5 PASS at Hermitian precision floor with
  `antisym/‖J‖ = {3.95·10^{−17}, 0.0, 8.98·10^{−17}, 5.05·10^{−01}, 5.39·10^{−17}}`
  for (diag-real-PD, diag-real-indef, Hermitian-complex, anti-Hermitian-complex iH, nl-Wirtinger) respectively. The fourth case correctly classifies the anti-Hermitian reference as asymmetric at the macroscopic level. The fifth case reproduces Lemma 2A.3-2 in silico — the Wirtinger-split nonlinear JVP is numerically real-self-adjoint at the Hermitian floor. Live six-block sweep on user local machine (PyTorch + BCC backend) is pending.

### [Infrastructure]
- None. No new dependencies. Numpy-only fallback is active when torch is unavailable (sandbox self-test path).

### Retires
- None yet. Steps (D2)–(D5) will, if the diagnostic localises the signal to $F_{\mathrm{cII}}$ and S1 proves correct, motivate a v2.6+ re-specification of the Class-II term either as (a) an EL-variation of an explicit scalar functional, or (b) a symmetrised `(F_cII + F_cII^{\dagger})/2` operator on the solver inner-product side.

### Math note
`TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.2 → v1.3, §2A.3 addendum (Definition 2A.3-1, Lemmas 2A.3-1 and 2A.3-2, Remark 2A.3 suspicion window S1/S2/S3, Plan (D1)–(D5), Acceptance-gate implication, Scope).

### Residual behaviour
Phase D of the v2.5 continuation driver is still a PLACEHOLDER; the live diagnostic still terminates with exit $10$ / `Status: SKELETON_ONLY` until Task #104 (v2.6.0). The §2A.3 diagnostic is **orthogonal** to the v2.6 wiring — it classifies the Jacobian itself, independent of whether Newton–Krylov is driving it. Once Step (D1) localises the block and Steps (D2)–(D5) narrow the scenario, the §2D acceptance gate tolerance will be finalised and either (a) admitted as-is with a documented bound on the residual anti-Hermitian component, or (b) tightened to block-level symmetry before v2.6 can claim §2D compliance.

### Next live-run command (gated on user local machine, PyTorch + BCC backend)
```bash
python Tools/check_jacobian_blocks.py \
    --config PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json \
    --N 32 --mu2 -0.5 --n-probes 5 \
    --output results/step1_block_probe.json --verbose
```
Expected per-block stderr: five blocks report `antisym/‖J‖ < 10^{−8}` (Hermitian floor), one block (`cII` by the S1 prediction) reports `≈ 2.66·10^{−4}` (matching the full-residual signal). Any deviation from this pattern falsifies S1 and forces branching to S2 or S3 via Steps (D2)/(D4).

## [continuation_mu2_v25 v2.5.7 + tect_newton_krylov minor — Math63 §2A.2 Exception-Handling Policy (Task #108)] — 2026-04-22

### [Code]
- `PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py` v2.5.6 → v2.5.7. Three broad `except Exception` branches on the Math63 v2.5 live-execution path narrowed in accordance with Math63 §2A.2 addendum. Module header bumped and full Trigger/Evidence(A-D)/Decision(P1-P5)/Retires/Math-note block inserted.
  - **Line 299 (import fallback)**: `except Exception as _probe_err:` → `except (ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError) as _probe_err:`. Rationale: a `SyntaxError`, `NameError`, `TypeError`, or `AttributeError` raised from inside `tools.check_jacobian_symmetry` is a programming defect, not a missing-module condition, and must propagate rather than silently disable §2A routing.
  - **Line 488 (`probe_jacobian_cached`, the Layer-5 culprit)**: split into two branches per §2A.2 dichotomy. `except (AttributeError, TypeError, NameError, ImportError): raise` forecloses the concealment path that let `backend.residual_bcc` and the complex→real cast survive four releases each. `except (RuntimeError, ValueError, ArithmeticError, MemoryError, np.linalg.LinAlgError) as e:` retains graceful degradation for CUDA OOM / numerical overflow / singular-Jacobian conditions but now logs `type(e).__name__: str(e)` to stderr **unconditionally** (no `if verbose` gate) plus a last-6-frames traceback via `traceback.print_exc` on the first occurrence per run (counter stashed in the probe cache).
  - **Line 751 (per-point main loop)**: same two-branch split. `stagnation_reason` in the `ContinuationPoint` record now stores `type(e).__name__: str(e)` rather than the bare `str(e)`, so MANIFEST post-mortem survives the loss of stderr.
- `PDE/tect_newton_krylov.py` line 97 (mock-branch import fallback): `except Exception:` → `except (ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError):` with §2A.2 rationale inline. No module-version bump required (side-effect-free import guard, not on the solve critical path semantically).
- `PDE/v24_thresholds.py` lines 265, 313 **reviewed and retained unchanged**. These are logger guards wrapping exactly one side-effect-only `logger.info(...)` / `logger.warning(...)` call; their scope is explicitly excluded from §2A.2 (P1) because they carry no physics state and their intent — "never let logging break the solver" — is preserved in the policy scope remark.

### [Theory]
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.1 → v1.2. §2A.2 addendum "Exception-Handling Policy" inserted between §2A.1 (Complex-Hermitian Probe Policy) and §2B (Adaptive Inner-Solver Switch). New content:
  - Definition 2A.2-1 (Programming-error / runtime-condition dichotomy): partition of exception classes into $\mathrm{Exc}_{\mathrm{prog}} = \{\mathrm{AttributeError}, \mathrm{TypeError}, \mathrm{NameError}, \mathrm{ImportError}\}$ and $\mathrm{Exc}_{\mathrm{rt}} = \{\mathrm{RuntimeError}, \mathrm{ValueError}, \mathrm{ArithmeticError}, \mathrm{MemoryError}, \mathrm{numpy.linalg.LinAlgError}\}$.
  - Definition 2A.2-2 (Exception-handling policy): five policy points (P1) no catch-all, (P2) dichotomy respect — programming errors re-raise, runtime conditions may degrade but must log, (P3) unconditional WARN log with `type(e).__name__: str(e)` + context, (P4) last-6-frames traceback on first occurrence per run, (P5) import fallbacks tighten to `(ImportError, ModuleNotFoundError)`.
  - Proposition 2A.2-1 (Detection guarantee): any programming defect on the Math63 v2.5 execution path either prevents module import, raises at the first call site, or is logged at WARN with a full type+traceback — no defect class admitting the v2.5.0–v2.5.3 silent four-release concealment pattern remains admissible. Proof sketch follows immediately.
  - Scope remark: policy applies to `PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`, `PDE/tect_newton_krylov.py`, `PDE/bz_preconditioner.py`, `PDE/math56_constants.py`, `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py`. Logger-only `except Exception: pass` in `v24_thresholds.py` lines 265, 313 explicitly excluded because its scope is a single side-effect statement.
- Header change history v1.1 → v1.2 block added.

### [Docs]
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4 header v2.5.5 → v2.5.7; version history v2.5.7 entry added above v2.5.5 (with full Trigger/Evidence/Decision/Fixes/Residual-status block). §11 revision table: new top row summarising v2.5.6 → v2.5.7 + Math63 v1.1 → v1.2 § 2A.2 addendum, Task #108.
- No change to `Docs/status/TECT-Theory-Code-Sync.md` (this patch is defensive, not numerical-result-bearing).

### [Results]
- No new numerical results. Exception-handling policy is physics-transparent: no Newton iterate, no probe output, no MANIFEST field related to converged physics changes in value — only the failure mode of programming defects becomes visible rather than silent. Two patched files (`continuation_mu2_v25.py`, `tect_newton_krylov.py`) AST-parse clean. The detection guarantee is **machine-checkable** by grep: the only remaining `except Exception:` on the Math63 v2.5 live-execution path is the two intentional logger guards in `v24_thresholds.py` lines 265, 313, which are covered by the §2A.2 scope remark.

### [Infrastructure]
- None. Policy introduces no new files, no new dependencies.

### Retires
- Silent `except Exception as e: if verbose: print(...); return None` pattern at `continuation_mu2_v25.py:488` (Layer-5 concealment channel).
- Silent `except Exception as e: print(f"  ERROR: {e}")` pattern at `continuation_mu2_v25.py:751` (main-loop type-information loss).
- Broad `except Exception` at `continuation_mu2_v25.py:299` and `tect_newton_krylov.py:97` (over-broad import guards).

### Math note
`TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.1 → v1.2, §2A.2 addendum.

### Residual behaviour
Phase D is still a PLACEHOLDER. The diagnostic run still terminates with exit code $10$ / `Status: SKELETON_ONLY`. Task #104 (v2.6.0) is unaffected — this patch is a defensive precondition, not a blocker for solver wiring. The next live re-run will, in the event that any programming defect remains in the §2A probe pipeline, now surface it as a hard abort with a visible traceback instead of the previously silent four-release persistence.

## [check_jacobian_symmetry v2.0 — complex-Hermitian probe (Math63 §2A.1 addendum)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: The v2.5.5 shape-contract fix landed and the subsequent live Stage $[4/4]$ diagnostic run (`R-2026-04-22-001`) emitted

```
UserWarning: Casting complex values to real discards the imaginary part
  (Triggered at tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py:102)
```

together with an identical `antisym=2.84e-04` classification at every Newton iteration of every one of the six $\mu^{2}$ points. The uniform classification across $\mu^{2} \in \{-1.0, -0.8, -0.6, -0.4, -0.2, -0.1\}$ is not a physical observable of the Jacobian; it is a cast artefact.

**Evidence**:
- (A) `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py:102` in v1.2 reads
  `x_torch = torch.from_numpy(x0).to(device=device, dtype=dtype or torch.float64)`.
  PyTorch 2.11 silently promotes `complex128 \to float64` with a `UserWarning`; the imaginary half of the seed is discarded before any probe is evaluated.
- (B) `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py:112` generates probes via `torch.randn(..., dtype=x_torch.dtype)`, i.e.\ real-valued Gaussians after the cast. The probed operator is the restriction of $\mathcal{J}$ to the real subspace, not $\mathcal{J}$ itself.
- (C) `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py:148`, `:151` evaluate the Rayleigh quotient and antisymmetry norm through `torch.dot`/`np.dot`, i.e.\ the bilinear form $\sum_{k} u_{k} (\mathcal{J} v)_{k}$ rather than the sesquilinear form $\sum_{k} \overline{u_{k}} (\mathcal{J} v)_{k}$. For complex operators the former conflates the Hermitian and anti-Hermitian components.
- (D) The v2.5.5 live run confirmed all three failure signatures simultaneously: warning at $(5\text{a})$, constant-$\alpha$ uniformity at $(5\text{b})$, and inability to distinguish $\mu^{2}$-dependent Jacobian structure at $(5\text{c})$.

**Decision**:
1. Rewrite `probe_symmetry` in `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v1.2 $\to$ v2.0 with preserved dtype end-to-end. `numpy.complex128` inputs map to `torch.complex128`; `numpy.float64` inputs map to `torch.float64`. No forced cast.
2. Branch probe generation on `x0.is_complex()`: complex-Gaussian $\mathcal{CN}(0, \mathbf{1}) = (\mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{1}) + i\,\mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{1}))/\sqrt{2}$ for complex, real-Gaussian for real. Orthonormalise via the ambient-field QR decomposition.
3. Replace all inner-product and Rayleigh evaluations with the real part of the sesquilinear form $\operatorname{Re}\langle u, J v\rangle = \operatorname{Re}(\overline{u}^{\mathsf T} J v)$, computed via `torch.vdot` / `np.vdot`. This is the correct Math63 §2A classification in $\mathcal{H} = \mathbb{C}^{n}$ and reduces exactly to the v1.x real-dot form on real inputs (backward compatibility preserved).
4. Extend `_self_test` from 3 to 6 cases: v1.x coverage (real SPD, real symmetric-indefinite, real asymmetric) plus three complex cases — Hermitian SPD (diagonal lifted to $\mathbb{C}$), anti-Hermitian ($i \cdot \operatorname{diag}(1\ldots n)$ with $\rho_{i} \approx 0$ and large $\alpha$), and non-diagonal Hermitian with $\pm i$ off-diagonals (Gershgorin-SPD). All six pass in both numpy-only and torch-present modes.
5. Add `dtype_kind` and `inner_product` fields to the probe return dict for downstream diagnostic logging.

**Retires**:
- `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v1.2 line 102 silent-cast pattern (superseded by `_preserve_torch_dtype`).
- `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v1.2 lines 148/151 bilinear `dot` calls (superseded by `_inner` helper wrapping `vdot`).
- `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v1.2 line 112 real-probe unconditional path (superseded by dtype-aware probe generator).

**Math note**: `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.0 $\to$ v1.1 adds `§2A.1 Complex-Hermitian Probe Policy` — a complete re-statement of the §2A probe design on the complex Hilbert space $\mathcal{H} = \mathbb{C}^{n}$ with the sesquilinear inner product, the weaker \emph{real-self-adjoint} criterion ($\operatorname{Re}\langle u, J v\rangle = \operatorname{Re}\langle J u, v\rangle$), a backward-compatibility proposition to the real case of §A, and a numerical verification remark tied to the v2.0 self-test.

**Residual behaviour note (expected at next `R-*` run)**: After this patch, the probe will report the \emph{real-self-adjoint} classification of the true complex $\mathcal{J}$. Because the TECT GL residual with $|\Psi|^{2}\Psi$ is the Fréchet derivative of a real-valued functional, the predicted classification is \emph{symmetric} (either SPD or indefinite depending on $\mu^{2}$, with $\mu^{2} = -1$ expected to be indefinite), not asymmetric. If the next run still reports asymmetric, a genuine Jacobian anti-Hermitian component (from lattice anisotropy, numerical boundary artefact, or residual sign-bug) will be the cause and will require a further Math63 §2A.2 analysis.

**[Code]**
- `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` v1.2 $\to$ v2.0 — complete rewrite of `probe_symmetry`, 6-case `_self_test`, and sanitisation of the CLI layer.

**[Docs]**
- `docs/math/TECT-Math63-Solver-Redesign-v2.5.tex.txt` v1.0 $\to$ v1.1 — §2A.1 addendum with definition, remark, v2.0 probe definition, backward-compatibility proposition, and self-test verification remark.
- `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.3 — header bumped to v2.0 with complex-Hermitian policy note and self-test expansion.

---

## [continuation_mu2_v25 seed shape fix — build_seed_bcc factory (v2.5.5)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: The v2.5.4 attribute-name fix (`residual_bcc` $\to$ `residual`) landed and the subsequent live Stage $[4/4]$ run printed a *new* error at every fifth Newton iteration of every one of the six $\mu^{2}$ points:

```
Probe failed: Psi must have shape (3, Nx, Ny, Nz); using default
[Jacobian class not available; defaulting to FGMRES]
```

The Math63 §2A probe-driven {PCG, MINRES, FGMRES} routing therefore continued to degrade to unconditional FGMRES — for a structurally different reason, one layer below the v2.5.4 fix. Exactly as with the `residual_bcc` typo, the v2.5.3 honest-reporting contract is what made the defect audible instead of a silent fallback.

**Evidence**:
- (A) `PDE/math56_constants.py` `build_seed(N, mode="thermal", ...)` returns shape $(N, N, N)$ dtype `float64` (lines $308$/$312$/$319$ of that module) — the scalar-Brazovskii convention that predates the BCC backend switch.
- (B) `PDE/real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3.py` `_shape3(Psi)` at line $95$ enforces `Psi.ndim == 4 and Psi.shape[0] == 3`, raising `ValueError("Psi must have shape (3, Nx, Ny, Nz)")` on any other shape. This is the exact error observed.
- (C) The mismatch was masked for four releases (v2.5.0–v2.5.3) by the `residual_bcc` typo — `probe_symmetry` never reached the shape validation layer because the upstream `AttributeError` fired first and was caught by `probe_jacobian_cached`'s broad `except Exception` branch. The v2.5.4 attribute-name fix let control reach `_shape3`, which then immediately rejected the $(N,N,N)$ input.
- (D) `PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py:675` additionally force-cast the seed to `np.float64` via `.astype(np.float64)` — a downstream contradiction with the backend's mandated `complex128` dtype. The `_to_torch()` helper silently promoted `float64` $\to$ `complex128` with zero imaginary part, so this layer was non-blocking but physically incorrect: the initial iterate had no phase content.

**Decision**:
1. Add a new BCC-aware factory `math56_constants.build_seed_bcc(N, mode, sigma, complex_seed=True, seed=42)` returning shape $(3, N, N, N)$ dtype `complex128` with independent thermal noise in each of the three BCC family channels. Three modes: `"thermal"` (complex Gaussian, per-channel variance $\sigma^{2}$), `"cold"` (all-zero vacuum), `"minimum"` (channel-uniform real amplitude with small Gaussian perturbation).
2. `continuation_mu2_v25.py` v2.5.4 $\to$ v2.5.5: import `build_seed_bcc` alongside legacy `build_seed`; replace line $675$ `build_seed(..., sigma=PHI_0_DEFAULT).astype(np.float64)` with `build_seed_bcc(N, mode="thermal", sigma=PHI_0_DEFAULT)`; drop the `.astype(np.float64)` (the factory already returns the backend-mandated dtype).
3. Legacy scalar `build_seed()` is left intact — preserves the self-test at `math56_constants.py:367` and any future scalar-Brazovskii regression caller. This is an *additive* fix: no existing caller other than the v2.5 driver is affected.
4. Unit tests executed in-sandbox across all four modes (thermal complex, thermal real-only, cold, minimum), checking shape $(3,N,N,N)$, dtype `complex128`, per-channel variance $\approx \sigma^{2}$, imaginary content for complex mode, determinism across repeated `seed=42` calls, and legacy API preservation. All six checks pass.

**Retires**: `continuation_mu2_v25.py:675` call `build_seed(N, mode="thermal", sigma=PHI_0_DEFAULT).astype(np.float64)` (superseded in-file by `build_seed_bcc(...)`). No cross-file retirement; no other caller used this pattern.

**Math note**: `TECT-Math63.tex.txt` §1 module spec for `build_seed` was underspecified on return shape; v2.5.5 sharpens the BCC branch by adding an explicit `build_seed_bcc` factory that matches the active backend contract. Math63 specification is *extended*, not amended.

**Residual behaviour note**: v2.5.5 is still a **SKELETON**. `run_one_point_v25` Phase D remains the placeholder that prints `[PLACEHOLDER: Newton step ...]` and does not call `tect_newton_krylov.newton_solve`. What v2.5.5 fixes is that from the next run onward the probe will *actually execute* and produce real classifications $\{symmetric, indefinite, asymmetric\}$, and the probe's internal torch-cast on the `complex128` seed will be the next thing tested. The `Status: SKELETON_ONLY` exit-10 contract is unchanged — Task #104 remains the gating item for any physics-bearing claim. A downstream risk (Layer 5) is flagged: `check_jacobian_symmetry.probe_symmetry()` at line $102$ calls `torch.from_numpy(x0).to(dtype=torch.float64)` which may raise on `complex128` input in current PyTorch; if that surfaces on the next run, it will be handled as v2.5.6 (probe dtype policy decision).

### [Code]
- **`PDE/math56_constants.py`** (v1.0 $\to$ v1.1) — added `build_seed_bcc()` factory returning $(3, N, N, N)$ `complex128` with three modes; module-version header bumped to v1.1; in-file Changelog block added.
- **`PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`** (v2.5.4 $\to$ v2.5.5) — line $675$ replaced; import list extended with `build_seed_bcc`; module-version header bumped to v2.5.5; full Trigger/Evidence/Decision block appended to in-file CHANGELOG; MANIFEST writer's self-identification string and generation timestamp updated to v2.5.5.

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — header bumped to v2.5.5; MATURITY block updated with v2.5.5 note and Layer-5 residual risk flag; version history augmented with v2.5.5 entry. §11 revision history row added.

---

## [continuation_mu2_v25 Math63 §2A probe wiring (v2.5.4)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: End-to-end execution of the v2.5.3 driver terminated *correctly* with `Status: SKELETON_ONLY`, exit code $10$, and the honest MANIFEST per-point table — i.e. the honest-reporting contract from v2.5.3 worked as specified. The same clean run, however, surfaced a previously silenced defect visible at every Newton iteration of every one of the six $\mu^{2}$ points:

```
Probe failed: module 'real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3' has no attribute 'residual_bcc'; using default
[Jacobian class not available; defaulting to FGMRES]
```

i.e. the Math63 §2A Jacobian symmetry probe has never actually been running in any v2.5 release — the `AttributeError` raised at `probe_symmetry(...)` setup was caught by `probe_jacobian_cached`'s broad `except` branch, the cached classification was set to `None`, and routing silently fell through to FGMRES regardless of the true Jacobian spectrum. Prior to v2.5.3 this was invisible; v2.5.3's honest reporting made it audible.

**Evidence**:
- (A) `PDE/real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3.py:478` exports `def residual(Psi, params) -> np.ndarray`, returning the full BCC residual in `complex128` of shape $(3, N, N, N)$. Static AST enumeration of module-level `def` names in that file contains `residual`, not `residual_bcc`.
- (B) Every other active call site in the active code path uses the canonical signature `backend.residual(Psi, params)` — verified via repo-wide grep: `PDE/tect_newton_krylov.py:{700,785,811}`, `PDE/math46_c2_extractor.py:278`, `PDE/backend_consistency_audit.py:{124,208,210,251,312,376}`, `PDE/tect_actual_extractor_pt_v3.py:{385,386}`, `PDE/run_pipeline_n1.py:116`. No other file references `residual_bcc`.
- (C) `continuation_mu2_v25.py:381` was the sole offending site, a v2.5.0-era typo. The `probe_symmetry(residual_fn, x0, ...)` contract itself is satisfied by the canonical `backend.residual`: it takes a state array and returns a residual array; the probe internally applies random directional FD to build Rayleigh quotients — shape and dtype of the residual are transparent to the probe.

**Decision**:
1. Rename the residual reference on line $381$ from `backend.residual_bcc(x, params)` to `backend.residual(x, params)`. One-token change; no semantic drift. Annotate in-file with a v2.5.4 NOTE so the typo cannot return.
2. No companion changes to `scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1`, `bz_preconditioner.py`, or `check_jacobian_symmetry.py`: the probe plumbing, preconditioner code, and handoff script all conform to the canonical signature already.
3. Defer a broader audit of every `except Exception:` branch in the v2.5 driver (Task #106, future) — such broad catches are what allowed this typo to persist for four releases; the lesson carries over to Task #104's Phase D wiring, where exception enrichment will be part of the contract.

**Retires**: v2.5.0–v2.5.3 `backend.residual_bcc` reference on line $381$ of `continuation_mu2_v25.py` (superseded in-file). No cross-file retirement because no other file referenced the misnamed attribute.

**Math note**: N/A (solver plumbing; Math63 §2A probe specification unchanged — it finally actually runs now).

**Residual behaviour note**: v2.5.4 is still a **SKELETON**. `run_one_point_v25` Phase D remains the placeholder that prints `[PLACEHOLDER: Newton step ...]` and does not call `tect_newton_krylov.newton_solve`. What v2.5.4 fixes is that from the next run onward, the per-iteration console log will show a real Jacobian classification $\{symmetric,\,indefinite,\,asymmetric\}$ and the selected Krylov method will reflect the actual Jacobian spectrum rather than the silent FGMRES fallback. The `Status: SKELETON_ONLY` exit-10 contract is unchanged — Task #104 is still the gating item for any physics-bearing claim.

### [Code]
- **`PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`** (v2.5.3 → v2.5.4) — line $381$ residual reference corrected to `backend.residual(x, params)`; module-version header bumped to v2.5.4; full Trigger/Evidence/Decision block appended to the in-file CHANGELOG; MANIFEST writer's self-identification string updated to v2.5.4.

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — version history augmented with v2.5.4 entry and a short note on the probe silently degrading prior to v2.5.3 honest-reporting. §11 revision history row added.

## [continuation_mu2_v25 TypeError fix + honest skeleton-mode status (v2.5.3)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: With the $\texttt{Tools/}\to\texttt{tools/}$ rename (v2.5.1 plumbing) and the UTF-8 pin (v2.5.2) in place, Stage $[4/4]$ of `run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` finally reached the Newton loop. `[Point 1/6]$ $\mu^{2}=-1.000000\mathrm{e}{+00}$ aborted immediately with
`ERROR: ContinuationPoint.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'converged'`
and the handoff script then mis-reported `*** v2.5 DIAGNOSTIC: PASS ***` because Python exited $0$. A run with *zero* real Newton steps thus looked indistinguishable from a fully-converged 6-point sweep.

**Evidence**:
- (A) **Constructor contract violation.** The `@dataclass ContinuationPoint` at `PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py:221` declares `converged: bool` with no default — i.e. required. The in-function seed at line $396$ passed only `mu2=...` and `r=...`, so every call to `run_one_point_v25()` raises `TypeError` before the Newton loop begins. This is a pure dataclass contract defect; the Math63 specification is unaffected.
- (B) **Silently masked error state.** The main-loop `except` branch at line $662$ appends a failure record with `stagnation_detected=True, stagnation_reason=str(e)`, but the MANIFEST writer (pre-v2.5.3) hardcoded `Status: PENDING_LOCAL_EXECUTION` and omitted the per-point table. Downstream readers had no way to tell that every point failed.
- (C) **False-positive exit code.** `main()` returned `None`, `sys.exit(main() or 0)` was absent, and the handoff script's archival block used `$exit -eq 0 → PASS` — so any clean Python exit, even over a fully-errored run, printed "*** v2.5 DIAGNOSTIC: PASS ***".
- (D) **Deeper structural issue surfaced.** Phase D of `run_one_point_v25` (line $\sim 469-475$) is an explicit PLACEHOLDER that prints `[PLACEHOLDER: Newton step ...]` and never calls `tect_newton_krylov.newton_solve`. The driver is a SKELETON, not a physics run — this was hidden by the false-PASS path.

**Decision**:
1. **Minimum-impact TypeError fix.** Add `converged=False` at the line-$396$ constructor call. Annotated in-file with a v2.5.3 comment explaining that the real Phase D (Task #104, v2.6.0) will flip this to `True` only after `newton_solve` reports residual below `tol_newton`; in the current SKELETON build every point correctly remains `False`.
2. **Honest MANIFEST writer.** Rewrite the manifest block to classify each result into exactly one of $\{\texttt{converged},\,\texttt{errored},\,\texttt{placeholder}\}$ and emit a markdown per-point table (`# | μ² | r | converged | stagnation | reason | wall`) plus aggregate counts. If `overall_status == SKELETON_ONLY`, prepend a **DANGER / HONEST STATUS** banner pointing readers to the v2.5.3 module header and Task #104. No more `PENDING_LOCAL_EXECUTION` string.
3. **Tri-state exit code.** `main()` now returns $0$ on PASS, $10$ on SKELETON_ONLY, $2$ on FAIL/UNKNOWN. `if __name__ == "__main__":` closes with `sys.exit(main() or 0)` so the code is actually propagated to PowerShell's `$LASTEXITCODE`.
4. **Handoff script cross-check.** `scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` archival block is rewritten into three explicit branches: $\text{exit}=0$ → PASS (recommend `build_version_index.py` + `commit_v25_diagnostic.ps1`); $\text{exit}=10$ → SKELETON_ONLY (loud warning, suppresses the commit recommendation, surfaces Task #104); any other → FAIL (direct readers to the per-point table and to `Docs/status/OPEN-QUESTIONS.md`). The script can no longer label skeleton output as PASS.

**Retires**: v2.5.2 MANIFEST hardcoded `PENDING_LOCAL_EXECUTION`; v2.5.2 exit-code-silent `main()`; v2.5.2 handoff-script single-branch PASS logic (all superseded in-file).

**Math note**: N/A (solver scaffolding + honest-reporting contract; Math63 specification unchanged). Task #104 will land Math63 §2A/§2C/§2D/§2E execution and bump the driver to v2.6.0 once explicitly approved.

**Known residual (SKELETON)**: After v2.5.3, a successful end-to-end run returns exit code $10$ with `Status: SKELETON_ONLY`. This is the *correct* honest state until Task #104 is executed — the driver is plumbing-complete but physics-empty. Any user-facing claim that the 6-point continuation has been verified *must* wait for a v2.6.0+ run that terminates with exit code $0$ and `Status: PASS`.

### [Code]
- **`PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`** (v2.5.2 → v2.5.3) — single-character-level TypeError fix at the `ContinuationPoint` constructor (line $396$); MANIFEST writer rewritten with per-point classification and DANGER banner; `main()` promoted to tri-state exit code; `sys.exit(main() or 0)` wired at module tail. Module-version header bumped with full Trigger/Evidence/Decision block.
- **`scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1`** (companion) — archival block rewritten into three branches (exit $0$ / $10$ / other), each with distinct console output and explicit guidance. SKELETON_ONLY branch suppresses the commit recommendation.

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — header bumped to v2.5.3, version history updated with the four-part fix summary and the explicit SKELETON maturity flag. §11 revision history row added.

## [continuation_mu2_v25 UTF-8 locale hardening (v2.5.2)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: After the user-side `Tools/` → `tools/` rename landed, the v2.5.1 diagnostic finally reached Stage [4/4] and the 6-point sweep actually started. The Math56 constants self-check passed; however `python PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py` then aborted with
`UnicodeDecodeError: 'cp949' codec can't decode byte 0xe2 in position 971: illegal multibyte sequence`
at `base_params = json.load(f)`.

**Evidence**:
- `PDE/config_template_brazovskii.json` contains a UTF-8 em-dash $\text{U+2014} = \text{0xE2 0x80 0x94}$ at byte offset $971$ inside a physics comment (raw context: `...left r=mu2=0.26, giving omega(q0)=0.046 — effective shell mass...`).
- The file parses cleanly as UTF-8 (RFC 8259 §8.1 mandates UTF-8 for interchanged JSON); byte-level verification:
  ```
  First non-ASCII at pos 971: 0xe2
  Context (raw bytes): b'=0.26, giving omega(q0)=0.046 \xe2\x80\x94 effect'
  Full-file UTF-8 parse: OK
  ```
- On Korean Windows Python 3.12, `open(path, "r")` without `encoding=` falls back to `locale.getpreferredencoding(False)` $=$ `cp949`, which does not map `0xE2` as a lead byte in that context.
- Tertiary corroboration: this is the third locale-portability regression in 48 h (after `Tools/` case collision and `--mu2_list` CLI mismatch); all three are Windows-specific and none surfaced in the Linux sandbox.

**Decision**:
1. Pin `encoding="utf-8"` on the config read (line $\sim 528$) and on the MANIFEST write (line $\sim 631$) of `PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`. RFC 8259 conformance; not a workaround.
2. Sweep every `open()` on the v2.5 active execution path. All three `proof_results.json` writes in `PDE/tect_newton_krylov.py` (lines $1453$, $1495$, $1575$) already carry `encoding="utf-8"` — false-positive from a regex that stopped at `os.path.join(...)`'s inner close-paren. Confirmed by direct read.
3. Defer the remaining non-active-path unpinned `open()`s (`PDE/make_rank2_bcc_seed.py:470`, `PDE/hess_jump_audit.py:398`, `PDE/transport_extractor.py:1419+1473+1475+1477`) to a separate lint pass — they are not touched by Stage [4/4] of the v2.5 diagnostic.

**Retires**: v2.5.1 locale-dependent `open()` in `continuation_mu2_v25.py` (superseded in-file).

**Math note**: N/A (I/O robustness; Math63 specification unchanged).

### [Code]
- **`PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`** (v2.5.1 → v2.5.2) — `encoding="utf-8"` pinned on both `open()` calls; in-file CHANGELOG block appended with byte-offset evidence. Module-version header bumped to v2.5.2.

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — version-history updated to include v2.5.2 with rationale.

## [Root-cause resolution: `tools/` ↔ `Tools/` case collision on Windows/Python 3.12] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: While static-smoke-testing the v2.5.1 `sys.path` fix, the in-sandbox import `from tools.check_jacobian_symmetry import probe_symmetry` *still* failed with `ModuleNotFoundError`, even though both `tools/` and `tools/__init__.py` were confirmed on disk. Escalated to root-cause investigation.

**Evidence**:
- `os.path.isdir("tools")` returns `True`, `os.path.isfile("tools/__init__.py")` returns `True`, `os.path.getsize("tools/__init__.py")` returns $1159$.
- `os.listdir(".")` returns $\{\ldots,\, \text{Tools},\, \ldots\}$ — note the capital `T`. `"tools"` is *not* in the list; `"Tools"` is.
- `importlib.machinery.PathFinder.find_spec("tools", sys.path)` returns `None`; `PathFinder.find_spec("Tools", sys.path)` returns a valid spec pointing at `/sessions/intelligent-funny-cerf/mnt/Contents/Tools/__init__.py`.
- Mount type: `virtiofs` exposing the user's Windows directory `C:\Dev\TECT2\Contents` directly (NTFS is case-insensitive on lookup but case-preserving on storage). The sandbox and the user's Windows Python 3.12 see the same canonical directory name.
- Python 3.12's `FileFinder` enumerates via `os.listdir` and compares case-sensitively. Hence `import tools` can never succeed while the on-disk name is `Tools`.

**Decision**:
1. Do *not* attempt the rename from the sandbox. Virtiofs + NTFS two-step rename is non-trivial and git-in-sandbox is currently unable to read config (`fatal: unknown error occurred while reading the configuration files`). Hand the rename to the user via a PowerShell two-step (case-preserving) command.
2. Record the root cause in `docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4 and in auto-memory (`project_tools_case_collision.md`) so the pattern cannot re-bite across context resets.
3. Keep v2.5.1 as-is: the widened `sys.path` + fully qualified import is necessary and becomes sufficient once the directory is renamed on the user's side.

**User-side action (PowerShell, in `C:\Dev\TECT2\Contents`)**:
```powershell
Rename-Item -Path .\Tools -NewName __tmp_tools
Rename-Item -Path .\__tmp_tools -NewName tools
python -c "import tools.check_jacobian_symmetry as m; print('OK:', m.__file__)"
```
The two-step is required because `mv Tools tools` is a no-op on case-insensitive NTFS. The third line verifies the fix.

**Retires**: None (diagnosis only — no code changed by this entry).

**Math note**: N/A (infrastructure / filesystem convention).

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — addended with a `tools/` case-collision note and the PowerShell remediation.
- **`/sessions/intelligent-funny-cerf/mnt/.auto-memory/project_tools_case_collision.md`** (new, auto-memory) — permanent root-cause record so the `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tools'` pattern is recognized instantly in future sessions.

---

## [continuation_mu2_v25 symmetry-probe import fix + run_v25_diagnostic.ps1 CLI contract (v2.5.1)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: First full-path local run of `scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` (after the v1.2 BZ-preconditioner and v1.2 self-test patches) reached Stage [3/4] with all three self-tests green, but Stage [4/4] aborted at `python PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py ...` with exit code $2$ and two distinct defects:
  1. `WARNING: check_jacobian_symmetry not found.` — Math63 §2A symmetry-probe-driven solver routing was silently bypassed, degrading v2.5 to a plain FGMRES solver (specification deviation).
  2. `continuation_mu2_v25.py: error: unrecognized arguments: --mu2_list -1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1` — caller/callee CLI contract mismatch.

**Evidence**:
- The `sys.path` bootstrap at (old) line $59$ of `continuation_mu2_v25.py` inserted only the PDE/ directory itself (`os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))`); the repository root was not on `sys.path`. The `check_jacobian_symmetry` module lives in `tools/` — a sibling of `PDE/`, not inside it — so an unqualified `from check_jacobian_symmetry import probe_symmetry` could never resolve regardless of CWD or `tools/__init__.py` presence.
- `continuation_mu2_v25.py`'s argparse block defines `--mu2-start / --mu2-end / --mu2-step / --diagnostic` (line $440$–$473$); `--mu2_list` was never part of the contract. The `--diagnostic` flag already hardcodes exactly the 6-point schedule $\{-1.0,-0.8,-0.6,-0.4,-0.2,-0.1\}$ at line $504$, so the `run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` caller was passing redundant and unrecognized arguments.

**Decision**:
1. Widen the `sys.path` bootstrap to insert *both* the PDE/ directory (so sibling modules `math56_constants`, `tect_newton_krylov`, `bz_preconditioner`, `real_backend_pt_bcc_mixed_v3` continue to import by bare name) *and* the repository root (so the `tools` regular package resolves). Order-independent: the two inserts only prepend to `sys.path` if absent.
2. Promote the symmetry-probe import to its fully qualified form `from tools.check_jacobian_symmetry import probe_symmetry`. Enrich the except-branch message so a future probe-unavailable state is diagnosable (name + type + message) rather than a silent FGMRES fallback.
3. Drop the stray `--mu2_list "..."` line from `scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` Stage [3/4]; `--diagnostic` is self-sufficient per the v2.5.1 CLI contract. Add an inline NOTE comment in the script capturing the traceability so this regression cannot silently return.

**Retires**: v2.5.0 `sys.path` bootstrap; v2.5.0 unqualified `check_jacobian_symmetry` import; run_v25_diagnostic.ps1 `--mu2_list` argument (superseded in-file).

**Math note**: N/A (solver plumbing — Math63 §2A probe routing restored, specification unchanged).

### [Code]
- **`PDE/continuation_mu2_v25.py`** (v2.5.0 → v2.5.1) — `sys.path` bootstrap widened to include repo root; `from tools.check_jacobian_symmetry import probe_symmetry` fully qualified; probe-unavailable warning now reports the underlying exception type and message. Module-version header bumped to v2.5.1; in-file CHANGELOG block appended.

### [Infrastructure]
- **`scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1`** — `--mu2_list` argument removed from Stage [3/4] invocation. Inline NOTE block documents the CLI-contract fix for auditability.

### [Docs]
- **`docs/manual/CODE_MANUAL.md` §10.4** — updated to v2.5.1; reflects new bootstrap + qualified import; records the CLI-contract removal in the companion handoff script.

## [check_jacobian_symmetry _self_test backend-coherence correction (v1.2)] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: First run of the v1.1 self-test via `python tools\check_jacobian_symmetry.py --selftest` raised `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for @: 'numpy.ndarray' and 'Tensor'` at Case 1.

**Evidence**: `probe_symmetry()` silently promotes a numpy `x0` to `torch.Tensor` whenever `_TORCH_AVAILABLE` is true, then calls `residual_fn(x_torch + ε u)`. v1.1 built the three synthetic Jacobians (`D_spd`, `D_ind`, `A_asym`) as numpy arrays; the residual closure `res_*` therefore attempted a mixed-backend `np.ndarray @ torch.Tensor`, which is not defined.

**Decision**: Choose the active backend at the start of `_self_test()` (`torch` if `_TORCH_AVAILABLE`, else `numpy`) and build `x0` and the three test matrices in that backend. Residuals now stay backend-coherent and the FD-on-linear argument remains exact to machine precision. No API change; only self-test harness rebuilt. Aside: `Test-Path C:\Users\jtkor\...\Python312\Tools` returned `False`, so the earlier case-collision hypothesis for the `python -m tools.xxx` ModuleNotFoundError was ruled out. The most consistent remaining explanation is a timing race between the in-sandbox write of `tools/__init__.py` and the user's next `python -m` invocation; the file is now confirmed present (1159 bytes) on disk.

**Retires**: v1.1 `_self_test()` (superseded in-file).

**Math note**: N/A (test harness).

### [Code]
- **`tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py`** (v1.1 → v1.2) — `_self_test()` backend-coherent; Trigger/Evidence/Decision docstring extended. Module-version header bumped to v1.2.

### [Docs]
- **`scripts/diag_tools_import.py`** (new, diagnostic harness) — One-shot probe of `sys.path`, cwd, filesystem view, and direct import attempts for `tools` / `PDE`. Retained for future namespace-import triage.

## [tools namespace-package fix + check_jacobian_symmetry --selftest — v2.5 diagnostic stage [3/4] unblocked] — 2026-04-22

**Trigger**: Second local run of `scripts/run_v25_diagnostic.ps1` (commit `badd11d`, after the v1.2 BZ-preconditioner patch): stage [2/4] passes cleanly, stage [3/4] fails with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tools'` on `python -m tools.check_jacobian_symmetry --selftest`, even though the sibling call `python -m PDE.bz_preconditioner` in the same stage succeeds.

**Evidence**:
- Both `PDE/` and `tools/` contained no `__init__.py` (implicit namespace packages).
- Only `tools/` failed to resolve — consistent with a case-insensitive-filesystem collision with the CPython installation's top-level `Tools/` directory on Windows / Python 3.12.
- Secondary defect: even if the import succeeded, the v1.0 `main()` of `check_jacobian_symmetry.py` had no `--selftest` branch — `--residual-fn` / `--x0` were `required=True` — so argparse would have aborted with `unrecognized arguments: --selftest`.

**Decision**:
1. Convert `PDE/` and `tools/` from implicit namespace packages to regular packages by adding empty `__init__.py` marker files (with an in-file traceability comment). This removes the dependency on filesystem-ordering-dependent namespace discovery.
2. Add an internal `_self_test()` to `tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py` that exercises `probe_symmetry` on three analytically-controlled linear Jacobians:
   - SPD diagonal `D = diag(1..n)` → expect `symmetric=True, indefinite=False, asymmetric=False`.
   - Symmetric-indefinite `D = diag(±1, ±2, ±3, ±4)` → expect `symmetric=True, indefinite=True`.
   - Asymmetric `diag(1..n) + tridiagonal skew(±2)` → expect `symmetric=False, asymmetric=True`.
   - Since the residual is linear, finite-difference reproduces the Jacobian exactly up to machine precision; thresholds remain at the production 1e-8 antisymmetry bound.
3. Intercept `--selftest` *before* argparse so the required-argument set is not enforced in self-test mode.

**Retires**: none. `main()` residual-function path unchanged.

**Math note**: N/A (tooling / namespace hygiene).

### [Code]
- **`PDE/__init__.py`** (new, empty marker) — explicit regular-package conversion; in-file traceability comment.
- **`tools/__init__.py`** (new, empty marker) — same, with cross-reference to this CHANGELOG entry and the `tools`-vs-`Tools` collision explanation.
- **`tools/check_jacobian_symmetry.py`** (v1.0 → v1.1) — `_self_test()` added; `main()` intercepts `--selftest` before argparse; `--selftest` documented in the argparse help block for CLI discoverability. Module version header bumped to v1.1.
